main_sections.title: The Chair
main_sections.prose_summary: Mario is a boastful boy who constantly brags about the enormous number of friends he has at school. His wise grandfather challenges him with a wager — a fruit chaat — betting that most of his supposed friends are merely companions or partners, not true friends. To help Mario discover the truth, grandfather produces an invisible chair from the attic, explaining that anyone who can sit on this tricky object will reveal who his real friends are through the magic it performs. Mario bravely takes the invisible chair to school at break time and attempts to sit on it in front of a circle of classmates. He falls repeatedly, to general laughter. On one final attempt, however, he finds himself seated and supported — looking around, he sees that three friends, Guneet, Asma, and Deepa, have silently moved behind him and are holding him up. The rest of the class did nothing but mock his falls. Mario leaves school with his three true friends and explains the grandfather's clever lesson: true friends are those who care for us and would never take joy in our bad luck. That evening, all four children visit grandfather, who has won the bet. They eat fruit chaat, listen to stories, and from that day on use the invisible chair test whenever they want to identify friends for life. The story is a light-hearted but pointed fable about the difference between genuine friendship and mere acquaintance, using the conceit of an invisible chair as a metaphor for the invisible tests of loyalty every friendship must pass.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Before reading: Write two words that come to your mind when you think of friendship.

Now, find some more words that are related to friendship from the grid given below. You can search horizontally or vertically. There are eight words. One word has been marked as an example. The first letter of each word has been given in bold.

question_bank.stem: Find eight friendship-related words in the word-search grid (search horizontally or vertically).

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Fill in the blanks to complete the words, which mean the same as 'friend' or 'friends' with the help of the meanings.

question_bank.stem: Complete the words using the given meanings: (i) a person with whom you do activities together — p __ r __n __ r (ii) an informal word for friends — b __ __ d __ __ s (iii) a person who you spend a lot of time with — c __ m p __ n __ o __ (iv) children who are in the same class as you at school — c l __ s __ m __ t __ s

tasks_verbatim.task_text: There are so many words for a friend. What do you call 'a friend' in your own language?
Let us discuss (mid-story pause after Part I).

question_bank.stem: Mario was very proud of having so many friends. State whether True or False.
What did the grandfather take from the attic?
Will Mario face any challenges?
Will Mario be able to use the magic?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us discuss (end-of-story questions).

question_bank.stem: Mario successfully sat on the invisible chair on his first attempt. State whether True or False.
Fill in the blank with a suitable word. Mario's grandfather challenged him to a bet of __________.
Match the following characters with their action: Guneet, Asma and Deepa — (i) held Mario up, so he wouldn't fall (ii) made fun of Mario's attempts

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following lines and answer the questions. (Passage 1: 'Take it. It's a very special chair…you'll be able to tell who your real friends are.')

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blanks with two words that describe the chair. The chair is _____________ and ___________.
Why was it tricky to sit on the chair?
Why does Mario's grandfather call the chair 'special'?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following lines and answer the questions. (Passage 2: 'Wait, wait, just a slight technical problem…Mario wouldn't give up.')

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blank with a suitable reason. Mario says there was a technical problem because ___________.
Mario's friends were very caring and helpful. State whether True or False.
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence. Mario wouldn't give up. This shows he is _____________. A. strong-willed  B. supportive  C. loving  D. hard-working

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Answer the following questions.

question_bank.stem: What was the bet about?
What happened when Mario sat on the chair for the first time?
How was Mario able to find his real friends?
Do you think the magic chair test was good? Why do you say so?
What does 'friends for life' mean to you?
The grandfather's idea helped Mario find his 'real friends'. Mention any other way that would help you find out who are your 'real friends'.

listening.transcript_text: When I first joined this new school, things were very difficult. There was Monika. She was a supportive friend who guided me, introduced me to others and even shared her notes. That made me comfortable in the school. Since then, Monika and I have been together through difficult times and happy times. She is the kind of friend who is always there for you. I'm really thankful to her, and how her help and support turned into a really good friendship.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to Manu talk about her friend, Monika. As you listen, fill in the blanks with words that you hear.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blanks: 1. Manu found it _____________ when she took admission in the school. 2. Manu met _____________ and was helped by her in many ways. 3. So, it was not so _____________ for the new girl to adjust in the school. 4. Both friends experienced difficult and _____________ moments together. 5. Manu is _____________ to Monika for making her life easy and finding a friend.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to the girl once again.

Work in pairs. Read the following qualities of a friend: loving, honest, kind, caring, loyal, polite, respectful, team-worker, understanding. Discuss in pairs. Choose any four positive qualities that you would like your friend to have. Give a reason for your choice. Use the hints given below when you speak: I feel my friend should be … because … / I think the quality I want in my friend is being …. The reason for this is … / I believe my friend should be … This is in order to … / If my friend is … then …

Read the messages that Mario has written to his friends Deepa, Guneet and Asma. Now, choose the correct word given in brackets to complete Mario's message to Guneet. Dear Guneet, I am __________________ (thankful to/requesting) God to have you as a __________________ (lucky/real) friend for life. When everyone was __________________ (laughing at/talking to) me, Deepa, Asma and you showed me that you __________________ (will save/care for) me. You are indeed a __________________ (blessing/loving) in my life. Best wishes, Mario.

question_bank.stem: Choose the correct word in brackets to complete Mario's message to Guneet.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Now, write your own message to your best friend. You may take ideas from the story, 'The Unlikely Best Friends' and the poem, 'A Friend's Prayer'.

Read the following words from the text: friendly, suddenly, talking, carrying, called, showed. Complete the following table by dividing the words into two parts (root word + suffix). One has been done for you: 1. friend + ly.

question_bank.stem: Divide the six words (friendly, suddenly, talking, carrying, called, showed) into root word and suffix. Complete the table.
question_bank.table:
Word|Root Word (Column A)|Suffix (Column B)
friendly|friend|ly
suddenly| | 
talking| | 
carrying| | 
called| | 
showed| |

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Match the following root words with suitable suffixes. You can use a suffix more than once. Use them to make sentences of your own. One has been done for you. Root words: magic, care, celebrate, laugh, wonder, help, friend, courage. Suffixes: -ing, -al, -ful, -ly, -ed, -tion, -ous, -ship. [Note: Observe the changes in spelling in a few word combinations.]

question_bank.stem: Match root words to suffixes and form words; then write sentences using each word. (magic–magical, care–?, celebrate–?, laugh–?, wonder–?, help–?, friend–?, courage–?)
question_bank.table:
Root Word|Suffix|Word|Sentence
magic|-ing|magical|The magician performed an amazing magical act.
care|-al| | 
celebrate|-ful| | 
laugh|-ly| | 
wonder|-ed| | 
help|-tion| | 
friend|-ous| | 
courage|-ship| |

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following lines from the story: 'Mario, brave and determined, took the strange invisible chair…' You have already described the chair. Now, describe what kind of a person Mario is in the image given below. [Use words from the story. You may also use words of your own.] The words that describe the quality of a person or a thing are called adjectives.

question_bank.stem: Write adjectives around the image of Mario to describe his character (using words from the story and your own words).

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Match each word (adjectives) in Column A with a suitable word (noun) in Column B. You can make more than one combination. One has been done as an example. Column A: brave, clever, tasty, heavy, slight, true. Column B: friends, rain, soldier, plan, peanuts, cold. Form Column C (phrases) and Column D (sentences).

question_bank.stem: Match adjectives (brave, clever, tasty, heavy, slight, true) with nouns (friends, rain, soldier, plan, peanuts, cold) and write adjective-noun phrases and sentences.
question_bank.table:
Adjective|Noun|Phrase|Sentence
brave|soldier|brave soldier|The brave soldier marched ahead.
clever| | | 
tasty| | | 
heavy| | | 
slight| | | 
true| | |

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Complete the paragraph with suitable adjective-noun pairings from the box: old book, magical adventures, happy boy, amazing stories, fantastic adventures, colourful creatures, old castles. Once upon a time in a little village, a/an 1. __________ named Abhishek found a/an 2. __________ in the attic. He read 3. __________ about fairy lands, 4. __________ and 5. __________. Abhishek's eyes got big as he imagined these 6. __________. He told his friends about the stories, and they started to have their own 7. __________ when they played together.

question_bank.stem: Complete the paragraph using adjective-noun pairings from the given word box.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Find out more stories about 'Friendship'. It could be from your state, other states or other countries too. You can share them with your class.
Describe your friends by filling in the blanks with suitable words. This is known as an acrostic poem. F ___ / R ___ / I ___ / E ___ / N ___ / D ___ / S ___

question_bank.stem: Write an acrostic poem using the letters of the word FRIENDS, describing your friends.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Complete the following task by talking to different people of different age groups as mentioned in the table. Ask: 'What does friendship mean to you?' to people in age groups 50+, 31 to 49, 20 to 30, 12 to 19. After gathering responses: Discuss similarities and differences across age groups. Reflect on how viewpoints on friendship may change with age and life experiences. Present a summary of the group's responses, emphasising key themes or unique insights.

question_bank.stem: Interview people from four age groups (50+, 31-49, 20-30, 12-19) on what friendship means to them. Record responses in the table.
